title: pdeq.json
description: Configuration file for a PDEQ install. Controls path resolution for specs, code, and nested/monorepo setups.

specsRoot.description: Relative path from this config file to the directory containing product/, design/, engineering/, qa/, and roadmap/ folders. Defaults to '.' (same directory as pdeq.json).

codeRoot.description: Relative path from this config file to the source code root. Used by the bootstrap analyzer. May point outside the pdeq directory.

platforms.description: List of platform IDs supported by this project. Supplements or replaces the platform table in CLAUDE.md. Each ID corresponds to a subfolder in design/, engineering/, and qa/.

pdeqDir.description: Path to the .pdeq submodule or framework directory, relative to the git root. Defaults to '.pdeq'.

nested.description: Configuration for nested installs (inside a monorepo package or feature subdirectory). Omit for root-level installs.

repoRoot.description: Relative path from this config file up to the actual git root. Used to correctly resolve symlink targets for scripts and .claude/commands.

label.description: Human-readable name for this component or module. Used in agent context messages to clarify scope. Defaults to the directory name.

examples.$comment: Root install — PDEQ and code live side-by-side at the repo root
Nested install — PDEQ lives inside a feature subfolder
Monorepo package install — PDEQ lives at packages/api/pdeq/
